channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-18214] ldlm_flock.c:221:ldlm_flock_deadlock()) ASSERTION( req != lock )
item.description:
MDT got the following ASSERTION at "flock" scaling test.  servers and clients are running master (commit:8011e33370)

[ 1912.913309] LustreError: 218855:0:(ldlm_flock.c:221:ldlm_flock_deadlock()) ASSERTION( req != lock ) failed: 
[ 1912.915070] LustreError: 218855:0:(ldlm_flock.c:221:ldlm_flock_deadlock()) LBUG
[ 1912.916394] CPU: 4 PID: 218855 Comm: mdt01_001 Kdump: loaded Tainted: G           OE    --------- -  - 4.18.0-477.27.1.el8_lustre.ddn17.x86_64 #1
[ 1912.920720] Hardware name: DDN SFA400NVX2TE, BIOS rel-1.16.2-0-gea1b7a073390-prebuilt.qemu.org 04/01/2014
[ 1912.922239] Call Trace:
[ 1912.922818]  dump_stack+0x41/0x60
[ 1912.923510]  lbug_with_loc.cold.8+0x5/0x58 [libcfs]
[ 1912.924413]  ldlm_flock_deadlock.isra.19+0x1fb/0x240 [ptlrpc]
[ 1912.925519]  ldlm_process_flock_lock+0x116f/0x3250 [ptlrpc]
[ 1912.926594]  ? lustre_msg_get_flags+0x2a/0x90 [ptlrpc]
[ 1912.927582]  ldlm_lock_enqueue+0x226/0x890 [ptlrpc]
[ 1912.928547]  ldlm_handle_enqueue+0x421/0x1750 [ptlrpc]
[ 1912.929555]  tgt_enqueue+0xa8/0x230 [ptlrpc]
[ 1912.930442]  tgt_request_handle+0x3f4/0x1a30 [ptlrpc]
[ 1912.931428]  ? ptlrpc_update_export_timer+0x3d/0x500 [ptlrpc]
[ 1912.932496]  ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x2aa/0xcf0 [ptlrpc]
[ 1912.933572]  ? lprocfs_counter_add+0x10e/0x180 [obdclass]
[ 1912.934578]  ptlrpc_main+0xc9e/0x15c0 [ptlrpc]
[ 1912.935463]  ? __schedule+0x2d9/0x870
[ 1912.936188]  ? ptlrpc_wait_event+0x5b0/0x5b0 [ptlrpc]
[ 1912.937146]  kthread+0x134/0x150
[ 1912.937803]  ? set_kthread_struct+0x50/0x50
[ 1912.938581]  ret_from_fork+0x1f/0x40
[ 1919.422438] Kernel panic - not syncing: LBUG
[ 1919.423416] CPU: 4 PID: 218855 Comm: mdt01_001 Kdump: loaded Tainted: G           OE    --------- -  - 4.18.0-477.27.1.el8_lustre.ddn17.x86_64 #1
[ 1919.425520] Hardware name: DDN SFA400NVX2TE, BIOS rel-1.16.2-0-gea1b7a073390-prebuilt.qemu.org 04/01/2014
[ 1919.427037] Call Trace:
[ 1919.427606]  dump_stack+0x41/0x60
[ 1919.428278]  panic+0xe7/0x2ac
[ 1919.428906]  lbug_with_loc.cold.8+0x2f/0x58 [libcfs]
[ 1919.429802]  ldlm_flock_deadlock.isra.19+0x1fb/0x240 [ptlrpc]
[ 1919.430891]  ldlm_process_flock_lock+0x116f/0x3250 [ptlrpc]
[ 1919.431946]  ? lustre_msg_get_flags+0x2a/0x90 [ptlrpc]
[ 1919.432911]  ldlm_lock_enqueue+0x226/0x890 [ptlrpc]
[ 1919.433852]  ldlm_handle_enqueue+0x421/0x1750 [ptlrpc]
[ 1919.434818]  tgt_enqueue+0xa8/0x230 [ptlrpc]
[ 1919.435669]  tgt_request_handle+0x3f4/0x1a30 [ptlrpc]
[ 1919.436623]  ? ptlrpc_update_export_timer+0x3d/0x500 [ptlrpc]
[ 1919.437653]  ptlrpc_server_handle_request+0x2aa/0xcf0 [ptlrpc]
[ 1919.438694]  ? lprocfs_counter_add+0x10e/0x180 [obdclass]
[ 1919.439668]  ptlrpc_main+0xc9e/0x15c0 [ptlrpc]
[ 1919.440519]  ? __schedule+0x2d9/0x870
[ 1919.441200]  ? ptlrpc_wait_event+0x5b0/0x5b0 [ptlrpc]
[ 1919.442126]  kthread+0x134/0x150
[ 1919.442750]  ? set_kthread_struct+0x50/0x50
[ 1919.443494]  ret_from_fork+0x1f/0x40

when test_5c "merging 20k flocks" performance-sanity.sh ran on 16 clients simultaneously, it hit problem.

test_5c() {
	touch $DIR/$tfile
	for((i=0; i < 20001; i++)) {
		echo "R$((i * 10)), 5"
		[ $i -eq 20000 ] && echo "W0,99999999" && echo "T0" && continue
	} | flocks_test 6 $DIR/$tfile
	rm -r $DIR/$tfile
}
run_test 5c "merge 20k flocks"

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment:
"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57060/
Subject: LU-18214 tests: modify test_5

{a,b,c,d}

for parallel
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 0b0e63e9c8467cabc32f0d2bd9b022cc33dad3ef

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56984/
Subject: LU-18214 ldlm: change flock deadlock detection
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_15
Current Patch Set:
Commit: 663bf05b6207089dbc400717c8e144fd40f9e7a0

"Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>" merged in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56319/
Subject: LU-18214 ldlm: change flock deadlock detection
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set:
Commit: c2e6fa41aac428222770a4fc2826567a74a6dbc6

"Yang Sheng <ys@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/57060
Subject: LU-18214 tests: modify test_5

{a,b,c,d}

for parallel
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 7f63bb18a92c4672d8ce7aed93246b7a905f9888

ys, can you please modify performance-sanity.sh test_5[abcd] so that it will run on all of the configured clients in the test session, instead of just client1. It looks these tests would force interaction between the different clients (and/or mountpoints), even if there is no "coordination" between the clients, because they are all locking the same file.

It may also be useful to extend these subtests to run with multiple mountpoints per client, if not enough separate clients, to increase the load. We probably want 4-6 clients/mountpoints to have at least some contention in the testing, but not so much that it will take a very long time to complete.

It looks like it is possible to save the generated command list into a file (e.g. $TMP/$tfile.cmd) on each client and then the test can read from the file instead of from stdin. Something like:

        do_nodes $CLIENTS flocks_test 6 $DIR/$tfile < $TMP/$tfile.cmd &
        do_nodes $CLIENTS flocks_test 6 $DIR2/$tfile < $TMP/$tfile.cmd &
        wait || error "flocks_test failed"

PS: please also fix these subtests to not use "rm -r" to delete a single file. Deleting $TMP/$tfile.cmd and $DIR/$tfile should be done with a stack_trap so they are not left behind if the test fails.

"Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56984
Subject: LU-18214 ldlm: change flock deadlock detection
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: b2_15
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 920c4820a65a4740f8a15652c9747ad6ca585f8e

we just hit this on 2.15.5 on both MDS's (after auto-failover).

seems like https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56319/ is pretty simple to backport to 2.15.x and I'll try that in our tree now.

can we please request that the final fix for this issue be backported to 2.15.x?
thanks

update patch https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56319/ seems to be working so far.
it passed test5_a, test5_b and test_5_c with not only single client, but 16 clients and 32 clients simultaneously.

I obtained a crashdump of this issue and it's confirming my suspicions. the crashing call to ldlm_flock_deadlock() is here:

                         /* Add lock to blocking list before deadlock
                         * check to prevent race
                         */
                        ldlm_flock_blocking_link(req, lock);
                        if (ldlm_flock_deadlock(req, lock)) {
                                ldlm_flock_blocking_unlink(req);
                                ldlm_flock_destroy(req, mode, *flags);
                                *err = -EDEADLK;
                                RETURN(LDLM_ITER_STOP);
                        }

we can also obviously see in the lock state that it's not really added to the resource (we can see the resource addition happens after the above statement)

the lock IS in the hash that we look for conflicts in (we just added it before the call to ldlm_flock_deadlock after all):

     {
      l_exp_flock_hash = {
        next = 0x0,
        pprev = 0xff229d874f34fe70
      },

 

Looking at the arguments, the req lock is asking for PW. the conflicting lock found is a granted PR lock, since it's granted obviously nothing is blocking it. We still try to see if anything is blocked by it and I guess something is, which we'll search and eventually come full circle to ourselves?

Which I think makes sense, we are essentially reproducing the classic deadlock scenario:

client 1: get lock1 (granted), ask for lock2

client 2: get lock2 (granted), ask for lock1

In this case the complication is probably that there's a gazillion of those granted read locks, but then there's a competing number of PW requesters equal to number of clients and they need to wade through those PR locks which with some probability would lead them back to themselves?

I think previous patch iteration was noted as causing the test to fail because flock enqueue failed, but I think this is not a test bug as the test was written to be run from a single client. Add more clients with multiple conflictign lock attempts and returning EDEADLOCK makes sense I think.

I don't think reprocess is actually a mandatory in here, none of the stacktraces seem to have it, but I agree on all other points.

sihara can we please get a crashdump from one of these crashes, it should clearly demonstrate what's going on to remove whatever little doubt is left.

I want to give a comment for the deadlock detection code. 

The test_5c in performance-sanity hit a assert in lustre flock detection code. It did a sample test:

type   start  end
read   0      4
read   5      9
read   10     14 
.....
read   200000 200004
write  0      99999998

 It intents to measure the performance for flock merging. But if we run it on multiple clients parallel, we got a LBUG. The  case can be simplified as below:

client1          client2

open file1       open file1
read(0,10)-lk1 <-----------------------enqueued
                 read(0,10)-lk2 <------enqueued
write(0,10)-lk3 <----------------------blocked by lk2
                 write(0,10)-lk4 <-----blocked by lk1. deadlock

if lk4 reprocess, then lk3 will be found, then lk4 will be found via lk2. The assert trigger.

 

We should determine such case as a deadlock. Otherwise those two clients will block forever. You can run below test on any filesystem to verify it.

t5() {
	echo -e "R$((i * 10)), 5\nP20\nW0,99999999"  | ./flocks_test 6 tfile
}

touch tfile
t5&
t5

 

 So the right solution is return a EDEADLK on client2 in such case.

"Mikhail Pershin <mpershin@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56818
Subject: LU-18214 ldlm: don't repocess flock in ldlm_handle_enqueue()
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: 92358609660ca9129223aabeaed5caf0fffd323f

"Yang Sheng <ys@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/56319
Subject: LU-18214 ldlm: don't reprocess for flock
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: a6762d107464b9d42829e711d7b235db8343adbe

Thanks Ihara. That datapoint works just as well for my purposes - while this is an important issue we will want to fix, it is not a regression in 2.16 so we do not have to hold 2.16 until it is fixed.

Peter, I haven't tested 2.15.5 particular, but I have an another datapoint (commit:6377859352) which is before landing LU-17276.
commit:6377859352 is also part of LU-17276, but that was test script only. So it's not related to LU-17276 codes itself.

For test_5c

test_5c() {
	touch $DIR/$tfile
	for((i=0; i < 20001; i++)) {
		echo "R$((i * 10)), 5"  <--- Add a read lock (offset_start=i*10, length=5)
==========================Since they are non-overlap, so we got 20000 read locks final.
		[ $i -eq 20000 ] && echo "W0,99999999" && echo "T0" && continue
===========Until last one loop(i==20000), Add a write lock(offset_start=0, length=99999999)
===========This lock will merge all of previous read locks have been enqueued.
===========It must cause looping on a long list, in fact, i thought old code should be 
better than new interval tree in such case. But in my test it doesn't. Of course, I just tested it on single client.
 
	} | flocks_test 6 $DIR/$tfile
	rm -r $DIR/$tfile
}
run_test 5c "merge 20k flocks"

Ihara

Has this test passed reliably on earlier versions of Lustre? Will it pass on 2.15.5, say?

Peter
